title: The G7’s Alignment on Iran and Ukraine Is Deeply Fragile | Council on Foreign Relations
gist: The 2026 G7 summit in Évian produced apparent transatlantic alignment on Iran and Ukraine, but the consensus is brittle: Europeans privately doubt the fast US-Iran framework deal, fear Trump will squander the Ukraine negotiating window by favoring Russia, and are moving to harden their own military and energy leverage including new sanctions on Russia's oil, gas and shadow fleet.

insights.claim: The G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, officially dedicated to global economic imbalances and China's subsidized exports, was instead dominated by the wars in Iran and Ukraine, producing a transatlantic alignment that is far more brittle than it appears.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: Headline G7 unity is a thin veneer; expect policy cracks that inject geopolitical volatility into energy and defense markets.
insights.quote: It is built on a framework agreement that the Europeans privately doubt and a Ukraine negotiating window they fear the United States will squander.
insights.locator: This year's Group of Seven (G7) summit

insights.claim: Europeans expect modest results from Trump's US-Iran framework agreement, worrying that its fast production and short negotiation period will make it significantly weaker than the original Iran nuclear deal and leave Iran's regional proxies unresolved.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: A weak Iran deal leaves re-escalation risk live, keeping a geopolitical premium under oil prices.
insights.quote: Europeans are concerned it may end up significantly weaker than the Iran nuclear deal and fail to resolve the question of the Islamic Republic's regional proxies
insights.locator: Undoubtedly, the announcement of a framework agreement

insights.claim: G7 countries appear to have reached consensus on new sanctions on Russia's oil and gas exports, especially targeting Moscow's shadow fleet.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: Fresh shadow-fleet sanctions could tighten Russian crude logistics and widen discounts; watch tanker and freight markets.
insights.quote: G7 countries appear to have reached consensus regarding new sanctions on Russia's oil and gas exports, especially on Moscow's shadow fleet.
insights.locator: Their idea is to ramp up sanctions pressure

insights.claim: The United States has indicated it may not extend the waivers it created during the Iran war energy crisis that allowed sales of Russian crude oil and petroleum already at sea.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: Letting Russian-crude waivers lapse removes a supply pressure valve, a potential upside catalyst for oil prices.
insights.quote: The United States has also indicated that it may not extend the waivers it created in response to the Iran war energy crisis that allowed for the sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum already at sea.
insights.locator: G7 countries appear to have reached consensus

themes.theme: NATO and US military posture in Europe

insights.claim: The Iran war's fallout led to US troop withdrawals from Germany, and the Pentagon and Trump administration are using the moment of European discord to push for reduced US conventional presence in Europe and NATO capabilities ahead of the July NATO summit in Ankara.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: A shrinking US footprint in Europe raises European defense-spending pressure, a structural tailwind for EU defense contractors.
insights.quote: the Pentagon and the Trump administration at large have used this moment of discord to push for a reduction of the U.S. conventional military presence in Europe
insights.locator: On the plus side, the framework agreement

insights.claim: The US's most important leverage over Ukraine is intelligence support, which Congress is trying to shield, and air defense interceptors, which have grown scarce because the Iran war depleted US global munition stockpiles.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: Depleted interceptor stockpiles imply sustained demand for air-defense and munitions producers.
insights.quote: air defense interceptors, which have grown scarce as the Iran war depleted U.S. global munition stockpiles

insights.claim: Trump's announced return to Ukraine negotiations stresses Europeans who fear US leniency on Russia could squander a battlefield and economic opening to pressure Moscow into an unconditional ceasefire.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: Risk that a premature US-brokered deal favoring Russia reshapes the Ukraine war trajectory and sanctions regime.
insights.quote: They worry that the United States' previously demonstrated leniency on Russia could once again undermine what they see as a moment of opportunity for Ukraine.
insights.locator: On the downside, Trump's announced return

insights.claim: Europeans must pursue a two-track approach of keeping Trump on board while dramatically increasing their own air-defense production and seeking co-production deals, exemplified by Zelenskyy seeking US licenses to produce Patriot missiles in Ukraine.
insights.speaker: Liana Fix
insights.so_what: Push for European and Ukrainian domestic missile production signals a multi-year defense-industrial buildout.
insights.quote: Zelenskyy is currently doing this by asking the United States for licenses to produce Patriot missiles in Ukraine.
insights.locator: This makes it all the more important
